summary:
\boxed{C} represents an error because chemical burns with sulfuric acid cause deep tissue damage via coagulation rather than producing singeing; singeing indicates thermal injury, not pure chemical exposure. This makes statement C false among all options presented as it incorrectly attributes sign findings to chemical burns when they should be associated only with heat-related injuries.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$ 

(Note: Final answer must match exactly what was selected by the agent.)

The correct answer is **C**, as chemical burns from sulfuric acid result in coagulative necrosis without typical singeing seen in thermal burns—singeing implies thermal trauma, so claiming its absence in such cases contradicts established pathology. Thus, stating that no singeing occurs in this scenario falsely suggests exclusivity and inaccurately characterizes the mechanism compared to actual pathophysiology involving deep tissue disruption instead.
